briefing:
# Atlantic Fintech Daily Briefing
### Tuesday, May 19, 2026

---

## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News / TechCrunch* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News / CNBC* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the collapse of fintech middleware company Synapse, highlighting serious risks in the Banking-as-a-Service model.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News / Workweek* — Fintech innovation is producing increasingly unconventional credit card products, reshaping how consumers think about credit and rewards.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News / FF News* — Apple has acquired UK-based open banking startup Credit Kudos, signalling the tech giant's deepening interest in financial data and alternative credit assessment.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News / Bits About Money* — A detailed analysis explores how open banking frameworks are reshaping competitive dynamics in the payments industry.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Canada Inflation Rises to 2.8% While Core Measures Ease](https://financialpost.com/pmn/business-pmn/canada-inflation-rises-to-2-8-while-core-measures-ease)** — *Financial Post* — Higher energy prices pushed Canada's annual inflation rate to 2.8% in April, its highest in nearly two years, though cooling core measures may give the Bank of Canada room to manoeuvre on rates.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News / FinLedger* — Canada's federal government flagged screen scraping as a security risk and committed to an open banking framework, a regulatory milestone directly shaping today's Canadian fintech landscape.

- **[ISC to be Acquired by Plenary Americas in All-Cash Transaction](https://financialpost.com/globe-newswire/isc-to-be-acquired-by-plenary-americas-in-all-cash-transaction)** — *Financial Post* — Information Services Corporation (ISC), a Canadian data and registry services firm, is set to be acquired by Plenary Americas in an all-cash deal valuing the company at approximately $51.00 per share, a 55% premium.

- **[Cohere acquires second company with German ties in Reliant AI](https://betakit.com/cohere-acquires-second-company-with-german-ties-in-reliant-ai/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian AI leader Cohere continues its acquisition spree, snapping up Reliant AI as it bolsters its enterprise AI capabilities ahead of anticipated competition in the financial services sector.

- **[Quantum startup Nord Quantique secures $1.4-billion USD valuation](https://betakit.com/quantum-startup-nord-quantique-secures-1-4-billion-usd-valuation/)** — *BetaKit* — Quebec-based quantum computing startup Nord Quantique has reached a $1.4 billion USD valuation, a milestone with long-term implications for Canadian financial cryptography and data security.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Irving family announces the passing of Robert K. Irving](https://financialpost.com/globe-newswire/irving-family-announces-the-passing-of-robert-k-irving)** — *Financial Post* — The Irving family has announced the passing of Robert K. Irving, Co-CEO of J.D. Irving, Limited, in Moncton, New Brunswick — marking the loss of one of Atlantic Canada's most influential business figures whose family enterprise has long shaped the region's economic and investment landscape.

- **[IMA Introduces Micro-credentials to Help Finance Professionals Build Practical, Future-Ready Skills](https://financialpost.com/globe-newswire/ima-introduces-micro-credentials-to-help-finance-professionals-build-practical-future-ready-skills)** — *Financial Post* — The Institute of Management Accountants is launching new micro-credential programs focused on AI readiness and data fluency, offering Atlantic Canadian finance and fintech professionals accessible pathways to upskill in a rapidly evolving industry.

- **[Inside ventureLAB's bid to bring AI to Ontario's critical industries](https://betakit.com/inside-venturelabs-bid-to-bring-ai-to-ontarios-critical-industries/)** — *BetaKit* — Ontario's ventureLAB is actively working to embed AI into critical industries, a model that Atlantic Fintech ecosystem builders could look to replicate as they seek to deepen AI adoption across the region's financial services sector.

---

*Curated by Atlantic Fintech | Supporting the fintech ecosystem across Nova Scotia, New Brunswick, PEI, and Newfoundland & Labrador.*
